pattern: The product's distinct prime factors are exactly the union of every individual number's prime factors, so factor each array element on its own (never the product itself) and collect the primes into a set.
hints: The product's prime factors are just the union of every individual number's prime factors -- you never need to compute the (potentially astronomical) product itself.
Since every value is at most 1000, trial-divide each number by increasing candidate factors up to its square root, recording any prime divisor found and stripping it out completely before moving on.
Collect every discovered prime into a set across the whole array; the answer is just the set's size.

edge_inputs.description: every element is a power of the same prime
a large prime paired with a composite sharing no factors
no single number carries every prime, but their union does
repeated identical values contribute only one new prime

stress.description: a large random array over the full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 10000))
    nums = [rng.randint(2, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
